_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in conducting quality review of serialized reports and reporting for time-sensitive USCYBERCOM operations.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in managing clinical data quality processes, including medical coding review, query resolution, discrepancy management, and the reconciliation of clinical data across electronic data capture, safety databases, and central laboratory systems.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-S-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-T-004 (RC, cosine: 0.468), DQ-S-004 (DQ, cosine: 0.448).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.8/10 (raw 0.584). Shared vocabulary: [quality, review, skill]. DCWF-only terms [conducting, operations, reporting, reports, sensitive, serialized] vs WIDAI-only terms [across, capture, central, clinical, coding, data] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.866 (P=0.871, R=0.861). Balanced P/R — the 14-word DCWF element and 31-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Skill in conducting quality review of serialized reports and reporting for time-" and "Skill in managing clinical data quality processes, including medical coding revi" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.486 < STS 0.584 (gap -0.097). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [conducting, operations, reporting] and [across, capture, central] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 16.7%. Shared tokens: [quality, review, skill]. DCWF-only: [conducting, operations, reporting, reports, sensitive, serialized]. WIDAI-only: [across, capture, central, clinical, coding, data].
